reasoning: The question tasks us with estimating the probability of the WHO declaring a new coronavirus pandemic in 2025. This market was prompted by the February 2025 publication of research on a novel bat virus, HKU5-CoV-2. A review of news up through June 2025 and beyond confirms there are zero reported human cases of HKU5-CoV-2, as the virus was only documented in bats. With zero cases as of late 2025, it is impossible for the WHO to declare a pandemic by December 31, 2025, because transitioning from an initial spillover to multinational spread and subsequent pandemic declaration takes months. Furthermore, no other novel coronavirus currently presents an active threat or has been declared a PHEIC by the WHO in 2025. The probability is effectively zero, bounded only by the 2% forecasting floor.
